cataloging_subject.label: Die Kildare-Gedichte (U-M main-text-and-appendix selection)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The exact bounded electronic selection represented by the pinned XML: Heuser's title page, the seventeen-component main text on printed pages 77-184, and the scholarly appendix on pages 185-229. It is not one medieval work, an authorial collection, the complete 1904 book, the complete contents of Harley MS 913, or a set of seventeen additional appendix works.

catalog_summary.title: Die Kildare-Gedichte (U-M main-text-and-appendix selection)
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous medieval authors, except Friar Michael Kildare for the first poem only
catalog_summary.date: Medieval texts in manuscripts chiefly of the fourteenth to sixteenth centuries; scholarly edition 1904; electronic selection 2006
catalog_summary.region: South-east Ireland, probably Waterford, for the principal Harley MS 913 witness; appendix sources are multi-regional

notes: The qualifier makes the electronic boundary explicit without pretending that the XML transcribes the complete 1904 volume.

titles.value: Die Kildare-gedichte; die ältesten mittelenglischen denkmäler in anglo-irischer überlieferung von Dr. W. Heuser ...

Old French comparison lines quoted in the editorial argument to Quindecim Signa ante Judicium; these editorial quotations are outside the 3,203 direct target-text verse-line accounting

form_statements.description: An edited selection combining verse, four appendix prose extracts, and scholarly apparatus; this display form is separate from the count-bearing item-form axis.
All seventeen count-bearing main components are verse-only.
Zero-item scholarly apparatus containing verse parallels and four prose source extracts.

subject_statements.label: Medieval Ireland and Hiberno-English textual culture
Textual transmission, variants, and philological editing

summaries.value: U-M deliberately encoded the title page, Heuser's main text on pages 77-184, and the appendix on pages 185-229, omitting the rest of the 1904 volume. The main text contains seventeen count-once poems; the appendix and nested parallel/version streams remain zero-item scholarly apparatus.

The principal Harley MS 913 poems range across devotional, homiletic, moral, satirical, political, lyric, and mortality verse. The appendix publishes parallel versions, analogues, four prose extracts, Tutivillus, and Piers Plowman C-text passages from sixteen physical appendix witnesses.

The extent uses nineteen mutually exclusive accounting records: seventeen main poem records, one parallel/version record, and one appendix-apparatus record. Structural wrappers and descriptive child parts carry zero metrics. The raw XML contains seventy-eight DIV1-DIV5 elements, so nineteen is not a raw XML division count.

extent.description: Nineteen mutually exclusive manifest accounting records: seventeen main poem records plus one parallel/version stream and one appendix-apparatus stream. The 17 poems are all verse-only. Direct target-text extent is 3,203 lines in 377 verse groups: 1,928 base-poem lines, 67 parallel/analogue lines, and 1,208 appendix lines. Eighty-nine editorial quotation lines are excluded. Four appendix prose extracts remain zero-item apparatus. Raw XML structure has 78 DIV1-DIV5 elements and is not represented by the sections value.

content_parts.label: Die Kildare-Gedichte U-M selection
content_parts.summary: Root wrapper for the title page, main text, and appendix selected for electronic encoding.
notes: Hierarchy wrapper; all metrics are deliberately zero.

content_parts.label: Title page
content_parts.summary: Front of the Heuser title page, encoded from U-M image 00000001.
notes: Hierarchy wrapper; all metrics are deliberately zero.

content_parts.label: Main text
content_parts.summary: Heuser's seventeen-component edition of the Kildare poems on printed pages 77-184.
notes: Hierarchy wrapper; all metrics are deliberately zero.

content_parts.label: I. Hymn by Michael Kildare
content_parts.summary: A 150-line hymn whose closing names Frere Michel Kyldare.

content_parts.label: Sarmun
content_parts.summary: The first of four separately headed sermon poems inside Heuser's Roman-numeral unit II.

content_parts.label: Quindecim Signa ante Judicium
content_parts.summary: A fragment of the Fifteen Signs before Judgment; editorial argument quotations are excluded.

content_parts.label: Fall and Passion
content_parts.summary: A religious poem on the Fall and Passion.

content_parts.label: Ten Commandments
content_parts.summary: The fourth separately headed sermon poem in Heuser's unit II.

content_parts.label: Seven Sins
content_parts.summary: One fragmentary poem across two XML sections and discontiguous Harley loci.

content_parts.label: Christ on the Cross
content_parts.summary: A fragment treated by Heuser as one poem across two cataloged incipits.

content_parts.label: A Song on the Times
content_parts.summary: A political and social poem across discontiguous Harley loci.

content_parts.label: Nego
content_parts.summary: A short main-text verse component.

content_parts.label: The Land of Cokaygne
content_parts.summary: The Land of Cokaygne, excluding seven verse lines quoted in editorial argument.

content_parts.label: Satire
content_parts.summary: Heuser's Satire on the people of Kildare; the editorial parenthetical does not prove the unnamed town is Kildare.

content_parts.label: Pers of Birmingham
content_parts.summary: A satirical main-text poem.

content_parts.label: A Rhyme-beginning Fragment
content_parts.summary: A twelve-line fragment also known by the modern title Repentance of Love.

content_parts.label: Elde
content_parts.summary: A mortality poem across two Harley leaves.

locators.value: f. 54v and f. 62r; IIIF canvases 116 and 131

content_parts.label: A Lullaby
content_parts.summary: The thirty-six-line Middle English base poem; its Latin translation stream is accounted separately.

content_parts.label: Lullaby Latin translation stream
content_parts.summary: Twelve Latin lines in two groups, structurally nested here but counted only in part:parallel.
notes: Descriptive child wrapper; metrics are zero to prevent double counting.

content_parts.label: Erthe
content_parts.summary: The forty-two-line Middle English base poem; interleaved Latin lines are accounted separately without duplicating the seven bilingual verse groups.

content_parts.label: Erthe parallel Latin stream
content_parts.summary: Forty-two interleaved Latin lines inside the seven base-poem verse groups, counted only in part:parallel.
notes: Descriptive child wrapper; metrics are zero to prevent double counting.

content_parts.label: Five Evil Things
content_parts.summary: The distinct seven-line Harley poem; Cotton and Rawlinson analogues are descriptive children and part of the separate parallel accounting stream.

content_parts.label: Cotton Cleopatra C VI analogue
content_parts.summary: Seven-line comparison version attributed by Heuser to Cotton Cleopatra C VI f. 22.
notes: Descriptive child wrapper; the seven lines and one group are counted only in part:parallel.

content_parts.label: Rawlinson poet. 32 analogue
content_parts.summary: Six-line analogue mediated through Wright and Halliwell's Reliquiae Antiquae.
notes: Descriptive child wrapper; the six lines and one group are counted only in part:parallel.

content_parts.label: Parallel translations and analogue versions
content_parts.summary: One mutually exclusive accounting stream for the nested Lullaby and Erthe Latin lines and the Cotton and Rawlinson analogues.
notes: Twelve Lullaby Latin lines in two groups, forty-two Erthe Latin lines in zero additional groups because they are interleaved in seven base groups, and seven Cotton plus six Rawlinson lines in two groups.

content_parts.label: Scholarly appendix
content_parts.summary: Versions of selected poems and previously unused Anglo-Irish sources on printed pages 185-229.
notes: Hierarchy wrapper; all metrics are deliberately zero.

content_parts.label: Appendix target-text accounting stream
content_parts.summary: One mutually exclusive zero-item apparatus record for all appendix verse and prose source publications.
notes: All 1,208 appendix L elements and 66 LG elements count as serialized target-text extent but create no additional intellectual item. Four prose extracts likewise create no prose-only item.

content_parts.label: Appendix part 1: versions of selected poems
content_parts.summary: Eleven scoped witness-version publications on printed pages 185-215.
notes: Descriptive wrapper; its 1,001 verse lines are counted only in part:appendix-accounting.

content_parts.label: Commandments and Seven Sins versions
content_parts.summary: Selected versions from Laud Misc. 463, disputed Harley 1706, Arundel 20, and Laud Misc. 416.
notes: Descriptive wrapper; all metrics are zero.

content_parts.label: Christ-on-the-Cross analogues
content_parts.summary: Six selected analogues from New College 88, Bodl. 42, Durham A.III.12 through Furnivall, Arundel 507, and Add 31042.
notes: Descriptive wrapper; all metrics are zero. The Durham route is indirect through Furnivall.

content_parts.label: Lullay
content_parts.summary: Thirty-six-line Harley 7358 version at f. 12v.
notes: Descriptive wrapper; all metrics are zero.

content_parts.label: Earth
content_parts.summary: Eighty-two-line Cambridge University Library Ii.4.9 version.
notes: Descriptive wrapper; all metrics are zero.

content_parts.label: Appendix part 2: previously unused Anglo-Irish sources
content_parts.summary: Four prose extracts and five Douce 104 verse extracts on printed pages 215-229.
notes: Descriptive wrapper; all metrics are zero.

content_parts.label: Four prose source extracts
content_parts.summary: Conquest of Ireland and Secreta Secretorum excerpts from Lambeth 598, 633, and 623 and Bodleian Laud Misc. 526.
notes: Complete source publications inside the appendix, but zero newly cataloged prose works.

content_parts.label: Douce 104 extracts
content_parts.summary: Tutivillus and four Piers Plowman C-text extracts from one physical manuscript witness.
notes: Descriptive wrapper; all 207 verse lines are counted only in part:appendix-accounting.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME XML for ajt2514.
evidence.locator: HEADER, title page, main DIV1, appendix DIV1, and all descendants
evidence.summary: Supplies the exact repository artifact, U-M identifier, source statement, electronic editorial declaration, selected text, hierarchy, page links, language evidence, and all content-part boundaries.

evidence.citation: Count-once content-part, XML-node, and direct-target-line audit of ajt2514.
evidence.locator: All 78 DIV1-DIV5 nodes, 377 LG, 3,292 L, and 19 mutually exclusive accounting records
evidence.summary: Verifies 17 count-bearing verse items, 377 direct-target verse groups, and 3,203 direct-target lines. The line total excludes 89 editorial quotation lines and includes 67 parallel or analogue lines plus 1,208 zero-item appendix lines. Structural wrappers carry zero metrics.

evidence.citation: ajt2514 lineage evidence for Heuser's 1904 edition and complete scan.
evidence.summary: Supports title, editor, edition boundary, main headings and printed pages, appendix structure, direct and indirect source statements, and editorial practices.

evidence.citation: ajt2514 lineage evidence for the conceptual U-M item, raw package, raw XML member, coding instructions, and repository normalization comparison.
evidence.summary: Supports the title/main/appendix selection boundary, 240-image continuity, exact raw member identity, normalized equivalence of raw and pinned XML, and the separation of current, raw, and repository layers.

evidence.citation: ajt2514 lineage evidence for Harley MS 913 and the main and appendix witness network.
evidence.summary: Supports witness identities and loci, the single-poem Michael attribution, south-east Irish principal-witness origin, direct and indirect source routes, facsimile coverage, and the unresolved Harley 1706 conflict.

open_questions.question: What manuscript and locus underlie Heuser's Harley 1706 appendix attribution at fol. 306, given the current 216-folio parchment description and nonmatching contents?
next_steps: Ask British Library staff to reconcile Heuser's paper, fifteenth-century, fol. 306 claim with current Harley 1706 records.
Inspect catalog history and the manuscript; do not silently substitute Harley 5396.

open_questions.question: How should component-specific composition places and anonymous authorship be refined without generalizing the probable Waterford origin of Harley MS 913 or Michael Kildare's single explicit attribution?
next_steps: Consult recent linguistic, codicological, and literary scholarship component by component.
Keep witness origin, composition place, conventional Kildare labeling, and named authorship separate.

notes: The conventional sixteen-poem count omits the seven-line Five Evil Things at Harley MS 913 f. 6v; Heuser/U-M structure, the BL contents record, UCC's modern edition, and Sinisi's explicit explanation support seventeen components for this electronic selection.

The content hierarchy is intentionally richer than the nineteen-record accounting axis. Every wrapper and descriptive child has zero item and verse metrics, so only the seventeen main records, part:parallel, and part:appendix-accounting contribute to extent totals.
Appendix completeness does not create new cataloged works: its versions, source publications, and four prose extracts remain scholarly apparatus within this bounded edited selection.
